pages.raw_text:
At the last meeting of the Cooks
union five new members were mi
tiated.


The Indiana supreme court has de-
cided that the weekly pay law is con-
stitutional.


The electricians, fathers and stone
masons will NEE at Central Labor
hall tonight.


Cooks union. No. $50. will not hold
another meeting until January 8. 1t
meets at 1857 Howard st.


The difficulty between the Wait
resses alliance and the Woman's EX-
change was settled this morning.


The Spokane Typographical union
has taxed it's members cents per
month to keep up the fight against
the Los Angeles Times.


A colored washerwomens and
cooks union is being organized in
Nalosta, Ga, by two white men. Con-
sternation prevails there.


Next meeting Of the Womans alli-
ance, No. 400, will be held on the
coming Friday at 1857 Howard St,
instead of on the customary night.


Tho shnglc wcavers are consider
ing the ACCESSIBILITY of forming a
union, and the trades council is doing
all within it's power to assist them.


The bartenders report that al
though their union is of very recent
date. it is now one of the strongest
in the city. Fourteen new members
were initiated at the last meeting.


c. w. Dow has replaced J. r.
Snyder on the arbitration committee.
Mr.. Snyder was unable to attend to
his duties on the committee on ac-
count of pressing duties elsewhere.
